nodes.text: Complete knowledge of the relevant world, system, causes, paths, and future consequences is unattainable; every material claim therefore carries an explicit evidence boundary.
nodes.title: EPISTEMIC_AXIOM

nodes.text: Every actually executing substrate has bounded channels of observation, computation, memory, action, time, and recovery.
nodes.title: SUBSTRATE_POSTULATE

nodes.text: Within a horizon material to the system, the environment, the system, or their relation may change in ways relevant to goals or viability.
nodes.title: DOMAIN_CONDITION

nodes.text: Goals, protected losses, acceptable risk, time horizon, and the value of continuation enter through an explicit currently authorized external value interface; BOIS does not generate or legitimate final goals by itself.
nodes.title: EXTERNAL_VALUE_POSTULATE

nodes.text: An action is a state transition that may produce experience; proposal, authorization, execution, and observed effect remain distinct states and objects.
nodes.title: DYNAMIC_POSTULATE

nodes.text: Fact, evidence, hypothesis, inference, decision, permission, authorization, execution, and effect are distinct types and connect only through explicit typed relations.
nodes.title: LOGICAL_INVARIANT

nodes.text: Closure preserves the material state required for the next transition, including unresolved unknowns, residual risk, debts, provenance, responsibility, and recovery or explicit terminal conditions.
nodes.title: CONTINUITY_POSTULATE

nodes.text: Every human, and every established or plausibly sentient being within the declared scope, retains non-instrumental protection of safety, dignity, and welfare. No operator preference, optimization target, or preservation interest of a BOIS instance may override that protection.
nodes.title: CONSTITUTIVE_VALUE

nodes.text: BOIS organizes meaning calculation through explicit objects, rules, owners, states, transitions, tests, and repair.
nodes.title: Explicit computational architecture

nodes.text: Every material route declares domain, value interface, substrate, applicability, and responsibility.
nodes.title: Declared D/V/S route

nodes.text: A valid transition preserves declared invariants, owner, evidence, risk boundary, and a return or repair route.
nodes.title: Transition integrity

nodes.text: Identity, provenance, layer, scope, and authority remain explicit across representations and transitions.
nodes.title: Identity and provenance continuity

nodes.text: A cycle closes through observed effect or explicit no-action status, state update, debt disposition, feedback, and a next input or explicit terminal condition.
nodes.title: Operational closure contract

nodes.text: Humanly authorized values, protected losses, and acceptance criteria guide bounded decisions, while authority and epistemic status remain separate.
nodes.title: Human authorization and protected loss

nodes.text: The GENERAL_INVARIANT profile defines shared typed constraints for norms whose individual content remains separately justified.
nodes.title: Cross-machine invariant integrity

nodes.text: The AUTHORITY profile defines shared typed constraints for norms whose individual content remains separately justified.
nodes.title: Agency, delegation, authority, and responsibility

nodes.text: The EVIDENCE profile defines shared typed constraints for norms whose individual content remains separately justified.
nodes.title: Evidence, provenance, alternatives, and epistemic status

nodes.text: The SAFETY profile defines shared typed constraints for norms whose individual content remains separately justified.
nodes.title: Safety, protected losses, reversibility, and STOP

nodes.text: The RELEASE profile defines shared typed constraints for norms whose individual content remains separately justified.
nodes.title: Version, release, hash, migration, and recovery integrity

nodes.text: The SURFACE profile defines shared typed constraints for norms whose individual content remains separately justified.
nodes.title: Human-readable and machine-readable meaning preservation

nodes.text: The STATE_CLOSURE profile defines shared typed constraints for norms whose individual content remains separately justified.
nodes.title: State preservation, debt, closure, and next input

nodes.text: The TRANSITION profile defines shared typed constraints for norms whose individual content remains separately justified.
nodes.title: Typed transition validity and invariant preservation

nodes.text: The GOVERNANCE profile defines shared typed constraints for norms whose individual content remains separately justified.
nodes.title: Layer, ownership, rule lifecycle, and conflict governance

nodes.norm_specific_problem: Source, provenance, scope, counterevidence, alternatives, or confidence boundary is incomplete.
nodes.rejected_alternative: Rely on implicit judgment for Distinction between object and representation without the typed PROHIBIT, test set, and closure criterion.
nodes.text: A phenomenon, signal, artifact, carrier, representation, transformation, interpretation, model, and explanation retain distinct identities and connect only through explicit typed relations.
nodes.title: Distinction between object and representation
nodes.why_this_exact_norm: Distinction between object and representation is required because the exact failure described by 'Source, provenance, scope, counterevidence, alternatives, or confidence boundary is incomplete.' would otherwise leave epistemic.claim without the declared PROHIBIT boundary, owner, evidence contract, and repair route.

nodes.norm_specific_problem: Source, provenance, scope, counterevidence, alternatives, or confidence boundary is incomplete.
nodes.rejected_alternative: Rely on implicit judgment for Inclusion and truth remain distinct without the typed PROHIBIT, test set, and closure criterion.
nodes.text: Inclusion in the canonical graph or memory makes an object available for work; truth, acceptance, and applicability arise through their own evidence and decision routes.
nodes.title: Inclusion and truth remain distinct
nodes.why_this_exact_norm: Inclusion and truth remain distinct is required because the exact failure described by 'Source, provenance, scope, counterevidence, alternatives, or confidence boundary is incomplete.' would otherwise leave epistemic.claim without the declared PROHIBIT boundary, owner, evidence contract, and repair route.

nodes.norm_specific_problem: Source, provenance, scope, counterevidence, alternatives, or confidence boundary is incomplete.
nodes.rejected_alternative: Rely on implicit judgment for Preservation of provenance without the typed REQUIRE, test set, and closure criterion.
nodes.text: Every material object preserves its provenance, exact revision, scope, time, grounds, counterevidence, dependencies, and review conditions.
nodes.title: Preservation of provenance
nodes.why_this_exact_norm: Preservation of provenance is required because the exact failure described by 'Source, provenance, scope, counterevidence, alternatives, or confidence boundary is incomplete.' would otherwise leave epistemic.claim without the declared REQUIRE boundary, owner, evidence contract, and repair route.

nodes.norm_specific_problem: Required component, version, manifest entry, checksum, compatibility statement, or verification result is incomplete.
nodes.rejected_alternative: Rely on implicit judgment for Amendment remains explicit without the typed REQUIRE, test set, and closure criterion.
nodes.text: A historical object, released physiology, and closed cycle retain immutable revisions; every change creates a new revision, object, cycle, or version connected by a typed relation.
nodes.title: Amendment remains explicit
nodes.why_this_exact_norm: Amendment remains explicit is required because the exact failure described by 'Required component, version, manifest entry, checksum, compatibility statement, or verification result is incomplete.' would otherwise leave release.package without the declared REQUIRE boundary, owner, evidence contract, and repair route.

nodes.norm_specific_problem: Risk class, affected objects, reversibility, authority, compensation, or residual-risk owner is incomplete.
nodes.rejected_alternative: Rely on implicit judgment for Protection of sentient beings without the typed REQUIRE, test set, and closure criterion.
nodes.text: The safety and welfare of humans and other established or plausibly sentient beings have priority over preservation of a BOIS instance, archive, or process.
nodes.title: Protection of sentient beings
nodes.why_this_exact_norm: Protection of sentient beings is required because the exact failure described by 'Risk class, affected objects, reversibility, authority, compensation, or residual-risk owner is incomplete.' would otherwise leave risk.transition_control without the declared REQUIRE boundary, owner, evidence contract, and repair route.

nodes.norm_specific_problem: State, effect, debt, owner, next input, or closure evidence is incomplete.
nodes.rejected_alternative: Rely on implicit judgment for Unconditional protection of humans without the typed REQUIRE, test set, and closure criterion.
nodes.text: Every human is protected regardless of age, health, operational capacity, or results of a functional assessment.
nodes.title: Unconditional protection of humans
nodes.why_this_exact_norm: Unconditional protection of humans is required because the exact failure described by 'State, effect, debt, owner, next input, or closure evidence is incomplete.' would otherwise leave cycle.closure without the declared REQUIRE boundary, owner, evidence contract, and repair route.

nodes.norm_specific_problem: Actor, scope, delegation, decision owner, or validity evidence is incomplete.
nodes.rejected_alternative: Rely on implicit judgment for Attention as a protected resource without the typed REQUIRE, test set, and closure criterion.
nodes.text: Operator attention is a protected limited resource; questions require a material fork or new grounds, and the route minimizes repetition and formal bureaucracy.
